steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The team's claim rests on a ten-year-old survey about willingness to walk, plus a general finding that sidewalks and crosswalks increase that willingness. The question asks which new finding best supports placing the bus stop farther away.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'sidewalks and crosswalks in the neighborhood have increased substantially over those ten years': More paved infrastructure means residents are now more willing to walk farther than the old survey showed, directly supporting the stop placement. ✓
  • Choice B — 'general weather behavior': Weather patterns don't speak to pedestrian infrastructure or willingness to walk to a specific stop. ✗
  • Choice C — 'cyclists use the route': About cyclists, not pedestrians; irrelevant to the bus stop placement claim. ✗
  • Choice D — 'rider satisfaction scores': Satisfaction with existing service doesn't address whether residents will walk farther to a new stop location. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
